title: What to Look Out for in Economic Data This Week (April 27-May 1)
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Watch for Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting, Q1 GDP, Core PCE, and labor market data for growth and inflation signals. Energy costs and inflation trends in Aus
excerpt: Watch for Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting, Q1 GDP, Core PCE, and labor market data for growth and inflation signals. Energy costs and inflation trends in Australia and Europe are also crucial.

content.introduction: Watch for Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting, Q1 GDP, Core PCE, and labor market data for growth and inflation signals. Energy costs and inflation trends in Australia and Europe are also crucial.
body: Inflation data for Australia is expected to run hot with the Middle East tensions remaining the main driver. In short, Q1 likely marks the beginning of a renewed inflation push rather than the peak and markets are looking for firmer price pressures ahead. Markets will focus on policymakers’ assessment of the recent pickup in prices, with headline inflation likely to move back above the 1-3% target band in April, driven by higher energy costs. ING analysts expect the BoC to keep rates steady through 2026, though the market is generally pricing a 25bps hike by year's end. As in other regions, higher energy costs in Europe are expected to push up headline inflation. The Bank is likely to highlight the impact of the energy shock, which is contributing to higher inflation alongside weaker growth. The report will be published after the BoJ meeting, and the Bank is expected to closely monitor it for confirmation that price pressures remain sticky.
Jerome Powell will take the lead for what is presumably his final FOMC meeting and press conference as Fed chair this week.
US macro data: Q1 GDP, Core PCE, and labour market data will provide key signals on growth and inflation dynamics. • Oil market developments:
keyTakeaways: Watch for Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting, Q1 GDP, Core PCE, and labor market data for growth and inflation signals.
